title: elaphrium fragile
intro: Elaphrium fragile, commonly known as Elaphium or Elaprium, is a flowering plant species in the family Gentianaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function. The therapeutic actions of Elaphrium fragile are attributed to its ability to act as a bronchodilator and anti-inflammatory agent, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as asthma and bronchitis. The bioactive constituents of Elaphrium fragile include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Elaphrium fragile can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing severe allergic reactions, causing skin irritation, and triggering respiratory problems in individuals with pre-existing conditions. Prolonged exposure to its extracts can also lead to nausea, headaches, and dizziness due to its toxic compounds. Furthermore, improper use of Elaphrium fragile may cause cardiac issues, including arrhythmias and increased blood pressure, posing a risk to individuals with cardiovascular conditions. In extreme cases, it may even result in kidney damage or liver failure if not handled and consumed with caution.
